info.title: LanguageTool API
info.description:
Check texts for style and grammar issues with LanguageTool. Please consider the following default limitations:
  • your daily request limit depending on your plan
  • maximum number of requests per minute: 20 (free) / 80 (Premium)
  • maximum number of characters per minute: 75,000 (free) / 300,000 (Premium)
  • maximum number of characters per request: 20,000 (free) / 60,000 (Premium)
  • for the free version, also consider the limitations documented here
  • Note: any parameters or outputs not part of this documentation are internal and must not be relied on
Need higher limits? See Proofreading API plans.

post.description: The main feature - check a text with LanguageTool for possible style and grammar issues.

text.description: The text to be checked. This or 'data' is required.

data.description: The text to be checked, given as a JSON document that specifies what's text and what's markup. This or 'text' is required. Markup will be ignored when looking for errors.

language.description: A language code like `en-US`, `de-DE`, `fr`, or `auto` to guess the language automatically (see `preferredVariants` below). For languages with variants (English, German, Portuguese) spell checking will only be activated when you specify the variant, e.g. `en-GB` instead of just `en`.

username.description: Set to get Premium API access: Your username/email as used to log in at languagetool.org.

apiKey.description: Set to get Premium API access: your API key

dicts.description: Comma-separated list of dictionaries to include words from; uses special default dictionary if this is unset

motherTongue.description: A language code of the user's native language, enabling false friends checks for some language pairs.

preferredVariants.description: Comma-separated list of preferred language variants. The language detector used with `language=auto` can detect e.g. English, but it cannot decide whether British English or American English is used. Thus this parameter can be used to specify the preferred variants like `en-GB` and `de-AT`. Only available with `language=auto`.

enabledRules.description: IDs of rules to be enabled, comma-separated.

disabledRules.description: IDs of rules to be disabled, comma-separated

enabledCategories.description: IDs of categories to be enabled, comma-separated

disabledCategories.description: IDs of categories to be disabled, comma-separated

enabledOnly.description: If true, only the rules and categories whose IDs are specified with `enabledRules` or `enabledCategories` are enabled.

level.description: If set to `picky`, additional rules will be activated, i.e. rules that you might only find useful when checking formal text.

200.description: the result of checking the text

buildDate.description: Date when the software was built, e.g. '2016-05-25'.

status.description: An optional warning, e.g. when the API format is not stable.

premium.description: true if you're using a Premium account with all the premium text checks

language.description: The language used for checking the text.

get.summary: Get a list of supported languages.

get.description: List words in the user's personal dictionaries.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset of where to start in the list of words. Defaults to 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of words to return. Defaults to 10.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Your username as used to log in at languagetool.org.

parameters.name: dicts
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of dictionaries to include words from; uses special default dictionary if this is unset

200.description: the user's words from the given user dictionaries

post.description: Add a word to one of the user's personal dictionaries. Please note that this feature is considered to be used for personal dictionaries which must not contain more than 500 words. If this is an issue for you, please contact us.

word.description: The word to be added. Must not be a phrase, i.e. cannot contain white space. The word is added to a global dictionary that applies to all languages.

username.description: Your username as used to log in at languagetool.org.

dict.description: Name of the dictionary to add the word to; non-existent dictionaries are created after calling this; if unset, adds to special default dictionary

200.description: the result of adding the word

added.description: true if the word has been added. false means the word hasn't been added because it had been added before.

post.description: Remove a word from one of the user's personal dictionaries.

username.description: Your username as used to log in at languagetool.org.

dict.description: Name of the dictionary to remove the word from; if the dictionary is empty upon calling this, it is deleted; if unset, removes from special default dictionary

200.description: the result of removing the word

deleted.description: true if the word has been removed. false means the word hasn't been removed because it was not in the dictionary.

message.description: Message about the error displayed to the user.

offset.description: The 0-based character offset of the error in the text.

length.description: The length of the error in characters.

replacements.description: Replacements that might correct the error.

offset.description: The 0-based character offset of the error in the context text.

length.description: The length of the error in characters in the context.

sentence.description: The sentence the error occurred in

id.description: A rule's identifier that's unique for this language.

subId.description: An optional sub identifier of the rule.

urls.description: An optional array of URLs with a more detailed description of the error.

id.description: A category's identifier that's unique for this language.

name.description: A short description of the category.
